questions.question: What is the difference between process goals and outcome goals in creative practice?

options.text: There is no difference—all goals are the same
Process goals are actions you control (e.g., 'Journal 10 min, 5 days/week'); Outcome goals are results you hope for (e.g., 'Eliminate anxiety')—build plans around process goals
Outcome goals are always better than process goals
You should only set outcome goals and ignore process

questions.explanation: Process goals (actions you control like 'journal 10 min/day') are more sustainable than outcome goals (results you hope for like 'cure my depression'). Build your plan around process goals—outcomes follow from consistent process. If outcomes don't come, adjust technique or seek professional help rather than just 'trying harder.'

questions.question: What is the recommended starting point for building a sustainable creative practice?

options.text: Start with 2 hours/day to see real results quickly
Start small (5-10 min/day, 3-4 days/week) and scale gradually—consistency beats intensity, most people fail by starting too big
Only practice when you feel motivated
Buy expensive supplies first to commit yourself

questions.explanation: Start smaller than feels necessary: 5-10 min/day, 3-4 days/week for Week 1-2. Gradually increase to 15 min/day, 5 days/week over weeks 5-8. Consistency beats intensity—better to sustain 10 min/day than burn out on unsustainable 2-hour sessions. Most people fail by starting too big.

questions.question: How should you handle the barrier 'I don't have time for creative practice'?

options.text: Give up—if you don't have time, you can't do it

Start with 5 min/day (0.3% of waking hours), audit where time goes (social media, TV), combine with existing activities, schedule like a meeting—if truly no 5 min available, life is in crisis-level overwhelm
Force yourself to practice 2 hours/day no matter what
Wait until life is less busy (someday)

questions.explanation: Solutions: Start with just 5 min/day (everyone has this), audit your day for lost time (social media, procrastination), combine with existing activities (journal during commute), schedule it like a meeting. If you genuinely cannot find 5 min, your life is in crisis-level overwhelm—address that first (therapy, boundaries, saying no).

questions.question: When should you seek professional therapy instead of relying only on creative self-help?

options.text: Never—creative self-help is always sufficient for all mental health needs

If you have persistent symptoms (2+ weeks severe anxiety/depression), functional impairment, suicidal ideation, trauma symptoms, substance abuse, or eating disorder behaviors—creative practice complements therapy but can't replace it
Only if creative practice makes you feel slightly uncomfortable
Immediately, without trying any self-help first

questions.explanation: Seek professional help if: persistent severe symptoms (2+ weeks), functional impairment (can't work/maintain relationships), suicidal thoughts, trauma symptoms (flashbacks, dissociation), substance abuse, eating disorders. Also seek therapy if creative practice consistently makes you feel worse or if working with trauma content. Creative self-help can complement therapy but cannot replace clinical treatment.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the 30-Day Creative Expression Challenge?

options.text: To master all creative techniques perfectly in one month

To build baseline habit (Days 1-7), experiment with techniques and collect data (Days 8-14), deepen core practices (Days 15-21), integrate and plan for sustainability (Days 22-30)—by Day 30, you know what works for you
To prove you can create every single day without missing
To create gallery-quality art by the end of the month

questions.explanation: The 30-Day Challenge builds in phases: Week 1 establishes habit (show up consistently, 5 min/day), Week 2 adds variety and data (try 2-3 practices, track before/after mood), Week 3 deepens core practices (drop what doesn't work, focus on what does), Week 4 integrates and sustains (solidify routine, plan Month 2). By Day 30, you have clear data on which practices work, what barriers you face, and whether self-help is sufficient.

title: Your Personal Creative Expression Plan - Quiz
